title: Urine sample preparation for untargeted MS-based metabolomic
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8hnss","text":"The following protocol describes the preparation of urine samples for non-targeted analysis by mass spectrometry coupled to liquid chromatography as proposed by the PFEM (MetaboHUB-Clermont).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"99boj","text":"A pre-analytical normalisation step is carried out based on the osmolality measurement determined for each sample.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ami4n","text":"The steps cover the sample preparation and quality control. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e5bbo","text":"The expected outcome of this protocol is a list of urine samples ready for analysis by LCMS.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2l7jh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"e0lc1","text":"Randomise the order in which samples are prepared and organize them according to this order.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ao23","text":"Two aliquots per sample to be analysed are required. The first will be used to determine the osmolality measurement and the second for metabolomic analysis by LCMS.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Thaw an aliquot of each Sample at Room temperature
Measure the osmolality value of each Sample using an osmometer. This setting determines the dilution factor that will be carried out on each Sample analysed by LCMS. Model 2020 Osmometer

Calibrate the device using the following three solutions (once per month) - 50 mOsm/kg (Advanced Instruments Reference :3MA005) - 850 mOsm/kg (Advanced Instruments Reference :3MA085) - 2000 mOsm/kg (Advanced Instruments Reference :3LA201)
Each day of use, carry out a quality control measurement (clinitrol) with a value of 290 mOsm/kg. If the measured value differs from the expected value, recalibrate the device.
Measure the osmolality value of each Sample . If it exceeds 2000 mOsm/kg, the Sample must be diluted in MilliQ water Contributed by users and analysed again.
Thaw the second aliquot of each Sample at Room temperature
Add 100 µL of Sample in Eppendorf tubes
Dilute each Sample in MilliQ water Contributed by users so that each diluted sample has an osmolality value of around 100mOsm/kg
A pooled QC sample is prepared by mixing 10 µL from each diluted samples
Each diluted Sample is divided into three aliquots : one is reserved for LCMS analysis in positive mode, another is dedicated to analysis in negative mode, and the last is a backup sample.
Store samples for LCMS analysis at -20 °C
creator: Estelle Pujos-Guillot
